blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Hey. I heard something happened after school.

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Who told you.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: It's a small school Larry lol. Are you okay?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. Someone pulled up at the right time.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Good. I'm glad.
She didn't ask more. Didn't press. That was the thing about her — she knew where the wall was and she didn't walk into it.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Can I ask you something kind of random

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: yeah

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Have you ever just needed to like, get out of your own head for a few hours? Just go somewhere with no homework, no drama, no school stuff?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. Today actually.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: There's a movie I really want to see. The 7 o'clock on Friday at the Regal. No pressure.
The phone felt heavier than it should have. I read it twice. Then a third time.
Friday. She was asking about Friday.
Thursday night. I got home, ate, sat in my room. The phone didn't do anything.
That was the thing about not having her number. Nothing to wait for. Just the quiet.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: bro you good? you've been quiet since the car drop

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Yeah. Just thinking.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: about Daniel?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: Among other things.
He didn't push. He let it sit. Sometimes Jonathan was better at silence than I gave him credit for.
